SEARCH
Package com.bencodez.simpleapi.valuerequest

Class InventoryRequestManager

java.lang.Object
com.bencodez.simpleapi.valuerequest.InventoryRequestManager
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.bukkit.event.Listener
public class InventoryRequestManager extends Object implements org.bukkit.event.Listener
Handles value requests that use an inventory interface for selection. Options are presented as clickable items. Additional buttons are provided for custom input and cycling the player's preferred input method.

    Description

  • Constructor Details

    • InventoryRequestManager

      public InventoryRequestManager()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public static void initialize(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin plugin)
      Register this listener with the plugin manager if not already registered.
      Parameters:
      plugin - the plugin used for event registration

    • openStringRequest

      public static void openStringRequest(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin plugin, ValueRequest request, org.bukkit.entity.Player player, String currentValue, List<String> options, boolean allowCustom, String promptText, StringListener listener)
      Open an inventory for selecting a string value. Options are displayed as paper items, with optional buttons for custom input and changing the input method.
      Parameters:
      plugin - the plugin instance
      request - the originating value request
      player - the player to prompt
      currentValue - the current value (unused in this interface but may be displayed via the title)
      options - list of selectable string options
      allowCustom - whether a custom input button should be provided
      promptText - the inventory title
      listener - the callback invoked with the selected value

    • openNumberRequest

      public static void openNumberRequest(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin plugin, ValueRequest request, org.bukkit.entity.Player player, Number currentValue, List<? extends Number> options, boolean allowCustom, String promptText, NumberListener listener)
      Open an inventory for selecting a numeric value. Numbers are displayed as paper items. A custom input button allows chat input for arbitrary values.
      Parameters:
      plugin - the plugin instance
      request - the originating value request
      player - the player to prompt
      currentValue - the current numeric value
      options - list of selectable numbers
      allowCustom - whether a custom input button should be provided
      promptText - the inventory title
      listener - the callback invoked with the selected number

    • openBooleanRequest

      public static void openBooleanRequest(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin plugin, ValueRequest request, org.bukkit.entity.Player player, Boolean currentValue, String promptText, BooleanListener listener)
      Open an inventory for selecting a boolean value. Two items represent true and false along with a button to change the player's preferred method. There is no custom input for boolean values.
      Parameters:
      plugin - the plugin instance
      request - the originating value request
      player - the player to prompt
      currentValue - the current boolean value (unused in this interface)
      promptText - the inventory title
      listener - the callback invoked with the selected boolean

    • openMethodSelection

      public static void openMethodSelection(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, Consumer<InputMethod> callback)
      Open an inventory that lets a player choose their preferred input method from the available list. Once the player clicks one of the methods, the provided callback is invoked with the selected method.
      Parameters:
      player - the player to show the method selection to
      callback - the callback invoked with the selected method

    • onInventoryClick

      public void onInventoryClick(org.bukkit.event.inventory.InventoryClickEvent event)
      Handle inventory clicks for value selection. When a player interacts with a request inventory this method determines the clicked item and invokes the appropriate callback or action.
      Parameters:
      event - the inventory click event

    • onInventoryClose

      public void onInventoryClose(org.bukkit.event.inventory.InventoryCloseEvent event)
      Clean up context when a player closes the request inventory. This prevents stale data and unintended interactions after the inventory is closed.
      Parameters:
      event - the inventory close event
